name: Suite 3 — Joins (Inner / Left-projection / Anti / 3-way / Transitive)
description: Canonical shapes 6 (inner-join projection 'X with their Y'), 7 (left-join projection 'X and any Y'), and 8 (anti-join 'X who never had a Y'). Deliberate emphasis on shape 7 to catch regressions if DefaultProjectionJoinKind config flips back to 'inner' — that option lives in CopilotOptions.cs:390 and is currently 'left'.

Scenarios.Question: tickets with their priority name
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, p.Name AS Priority FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: tickets with their status name
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, s.Name AS Status FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: tickets with entity name status name and priority name
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, e.Name AS Entity, s.Name AS Status, p.Name AS Priority FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: tickets and any assigned user (include unassigned)
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.TicketNumber, u.UserName FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.AssignedToUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
Scenarios._note: Shape 7 emphasis. Pre-DefaultProjectionJoinKind=left this would silently drop unassigned tickets via INNER JOIN.

Scenarios.Question: entities and any tickets they have
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT e.Name, t.TicketNumber FROM Entitys e LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.EntityId = e.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 WHERE e.IsActive = 1
Scenarios._note: Shape 7 emphasis. Tests that zero-ticket entities still appear.

Scenarios.Question: users with their ticket count including users who created none
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName, COUNT(t.Id) AS TicketCount FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.UserName ORDER BY TicketCount DESC
Scenarios._note: Shape 7 + aggregation. Common business question — Inner JOIN would lose the long tail of users.

Scenarios.Question: ticket statuses with their ticket count including unused statuses
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT s.Name, COUNT(t.Id) AS Count FROM TicketStatuses s LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.StatusId = s.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY s.Name ORDER BY Count DESC
Scenarios._note: Shape 7 emphasis. Newly-added statuses with zero tickets must appear with Count=0.

Scenarios.Question: users with no tickets
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.UserName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0)

Scenarios.Question: entities that never had a ticket
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT e.Name FROM Entitys e WHERE e.IsActive = 1 AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.EntityId = e.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0)

Scenarios.Question: priorities never used by any ticket
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT p.Name FROM TicketPriorities p WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Tickets t WHERE t.PriorityId = p.Id AND t.IsDeleted = 0)

Scenarios.Question: users who created tickets in the Engineering entity
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT u.UserName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE e.Name = 'Engineering' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: ticket statuses used by tickets in active entities
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT DISTINCT s.Name FROM TicketStatuses s JOIN Tickets t ON t.StatusId = s.Id JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE e.IsActive = 1 AND t.IsDeleted = 0
